Marsh (NYSE: MRSH) is a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information, visit corporate.marsh.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X.
Marsh is committed to creating a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age, background, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.
Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. title: Curricular Internship – Finance & Corporate Compliance
description:
Curricular Internship – Finance \& Corporate Compliance
  
  

Reversal is an investment firm (SIM) authorized to provide advisory and placement services, with TXT Group holding a majority stake.
  
  

To strengthen the Finance \& Corporate Compliance team, we are looking for a curricular intern who will support operational and control activities, reporting directly to the CFO.
  
  

The selected candidate will support the team in coordinating activities between internal departments and outsourced control functions (Compliance, Risk Management, Internal Audit), contributing to the proper functioning of internal processes and ensuring compliance with applicable regulations.
  
Key Responsibilities
  
  

The intern will support the CFO in the following operational and analytical activities:
  
  

Assisting in the mapping and analysis of business processes and operational workflows across different functions (Compliance, Risk Management, Internal Audit)
  
  

Supporting the preparation of periodic reports for regulatory authorities (Bank of Italy, CONSOB)
  
  

Collecting and organizing data for monitoring activities and regulatory compliance checks
  
  

Supporting the analysis and optimization of internal documentation and operational procedures to improve process efficiency and ensure business continuity
  
  

Assisting with internal control activities, verifying alignment between documented procedures and actual operations
  
  

Preparing materials and documentation for internal committees and audit activities
  
Requirements
  
  

Degree in Economics, Finance, Management Engineering, or legal studies with an economic focus
  
  

Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office
  
Soft Skills
  
  

Accuracy and attention to detail, analytical mindset and teamwork attitude
  
  

Interest in financial regulation and corporate organization
  
Why join Reversal?
  
You will join a fast\-growing and innovative company in the Italian wealth management landscape, where Reversal SIM is increasingly positioning itself as a key player.
  
Reversal stands out for its advanced approach to financial advisory, applying asset allocation and portfolio construction methodologies typically used by large institutional investors, combined with global expertise.
  
The company acts as an exclusive partner and distributor in the Italian market for the expertise and products of one of the leading global asset and wealth managers based in the United States: Northern Trust.
  
Joining Reversal represents an opportunity to work in a growing environment and to rapidly develop specialized skills through daily interaction with experienced professionals in the investment sector.
  
What we offer
  
  

6\-month curricular internship
  
  

**Monthly allowance:** €600
  
  

This position is open to candidates of all genders, in accordance with Italian Legislative Decree no. 198/2006\.
  
  

The company promotes equal opportunities and values diversity in all its forms.

title: HSE UPSTREAM COORDINATOR - PARIS
description:
HSE Upstream Coordinator
POSITION OVERVIEW


We are seeking an experienced HSE Upstream Coordinator. This is a critical safety and compliance role supporting upstream oil \& gas operations in Mozambique, with the primary work location in Paris for a minimum 12\-month assignment (renewable).


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


Health, Safety \& Environment (HSE) Coordination

* Develop, implement, and monitor HSE policies, procedures, and standards aligned with international best practices (IOGP, API, ISO 45001, ISO 14001\)
* Conduct HSE risk assessments and hazard analyses for upstream operations (drilling, production, facilities)
* Coordinate HSE audits, inspections, and compliance reviews across project phases
* Manage incident reporting, investigation, and corrective action tracking
* Ensure adherence to Mozambique regulatory requirements and host country legislation


Operational HSE Management

* Liaise with onsite HSE teams in Mozambique to ensure real\-time compliance and risk mitigation
* Support toolbox talks, safety briefings, and HSE training delivery
* Monitor Key Performance Indicators (KPIs): TRIFR, LTIFR, near\-miss reporting, and environmental metrics
* Prepare HSE reports, dashboards, and management presentations for stakeholders
* Coordinate emergency response drills and contingency planning


Stakeholder \& Vendor Management

* Interface with contractors, service providers, and supply chain partners on HSE requirements
* Ensure HSE clauses in contracts and procurement specifications
* Conduct HSE pre\-qualification assessments for vendors and subcontractors
* Manage HSE documentation and compliance records


Project Integration

* Collaborate with Project Management, Engineering, Procurement, and Construction teams
* Integrate HSE requirements into project schedules and budgets
* Support commissioning activities with HSE oversight and sign\-offs
* Participate in project meetings and steering committees


Environmental \& Regulatory Compliance

* Monitor environmental permits and regulatory compliance status
* Coordinate environmental impact assessments and mitigation measures
* Manage waste management, emissions reporting, and environmental monitoring protocols
* Ensure alignment with Mozambique environmental legislation and international standards


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS


Education \& Certifications:

* Bachelor's degree in Occupational Health \& Safety, Engineering, Environmental Science, or related field
* NEBOSH IGC (International General Certificate) or equivalent HSE certification (minimum requirement)
* IOSH Managing Safely or equivalent preferred
* Valid First Aid/CPR certification


Experience:

* Minimum 7\-10 years

 of HSE experience in upstream oil \& gas operations

 (onshore or offshore)
* Proven experience in project\-based environments

 (construction, commissioning, or major turnarounds)
* Demonstrated expertise in risk assessment, incident investigation, and compliance management
* Experience working in sub\-Saharan Africa or similar emerging markets

 is a strong advantage

  

Soft Skills:

* Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities
* Strong leadership and team coordination skills
* Analytical and problem\-solving mindset
* Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities
* Fluency in English

 (mandatory); French or Portuguese a plus


WORK LOCATION \& LOGISTICS

* Primary Base:

 Paris, France
* Assignment Duration:

 Minimum 12 months (renewable based on project needs)

title: Intern - Accounting Advisory Services
description:
*We let the future guide us. Join our next generation*


***Do Work That Matters*** **\- Il tuo ruolo e le tue responsabilità**



Vuoi fare un lavoro che sia davvero significativo e di impatto? In KPMG avrai l’opportunità di aiutare i clienti, la società, le colleghe e i colleghi ad affrontare e risolvere le sfide più attuali e complesse. Nel rispetto dei valori di integrità e qualità, **KPMG Audit \& Assurance** offre un portafoglio di servizi integrato in costante evoluzione, con insight che consentono alle aziende di rispondere ai fabbisogni informativi del mercato e degli investitori.



Il **Team Accounting Advisory Services**, grazie all’**innovazione**, si occupa di rendere le procedure di *assurance* conformi e attente ai **temi ESG**, sempre più **digitalizzate**, **integrate**, **automatizzate** e basate sull’ **Intelligenza Artificiale** e sui **Big Data**. Entrando a far parte del team, collaborerai con clienti leader di settore a livello nazionale e internazionale nel trasformare i processi e i modelli operativi e contabili, per renderli *compliant* alla normativa e performanti rispetto al business model dell’azienda. Il tuo ruolo sarà incentrato su strategie, Financial e Regulatory reporting e analisi delle piattaforme tecnologiche a supporto dei processi CFO e del cambiamento organizzativo.

  



Nello specifico, ti occuperai di supportare le attività di:


* *Finance Trasformation*: disegno del **modello contabile** e dei **processi**, definizione degli **impatti di business** ed **organizzativi** ed individuazione dei **requisiti target** a supporto delle implementazioni dei principi **IFRS** (es. IFRS 17 e IFRS9\) e/o a seguito di operazioni di **fusione/acquisizione**
* *Planning and Control*: revisione del **modello** e dei **processi di pianificazione e controllo**
* *Change Management*: supporto nella definizione dei nuovi **ruoli** e **responsabilità** all'interno dell'organizzazione, alla luce delle progettualità in essere; individuazione delle principali **novità operative** e **gestionali**; analisi dei principali **fattori critici** alla base del cambiamento
* *Technologies change e Smart Digital Finance*: sviluppo di **soluzioni/piattaforme** e **processi di automation** anche mediante l’uso di **Intelligenza Artificiale** e **Big Data**
* *Accounting advice in ambito finance:* predisposizione del **reporting finanziario** richiesto nell’ambito di progetti di **transaction services** o di **operazioni straordinarie**
* *Project Management*: predisposizione e monitoraggio del **piano delle attività**, coordinamento tra gli **Stakeholder** **di** **Business** e **IT** durante le fasi implementative, predisposizione **documentazione progettuale** per **Top Management**.

  


***Come As You Are \- Le tue competenze e caratteristiche***



Questa opportunità è perfetta per te se:


* Hai o stai per conseguire una **Laurea Magistrale** in **Economia**/**Finanza**
* Hai acquisito una conoscenza dei **Principi Contabili Italiani** e **IAS/IFRS**
* Hai un’ottima conoscenza della **lingua inglese**, preferibilmente avvalorata da esperienze di studio e/o lavoro all'estero
* Hai un’ottima conoscenza del **pacchetto Office.**


***Your Future With Us \- Cosa troverai in KPMG***


* Un ambiente di **lavoro inclusivo**, equo e che rispetta, incoraggia e valorizza le **diversità**
* Un contesto stimolante e dinamico che incentiva la **creatività** e il **lavoro di squadra**
* Modalità di lavoro **ibrida,** per promuovere un sano equilibrio tra vita lavorativa e personale
* Programmi di **formazione** e sviluppo avanzati per rimanere sempre aggiornati
* **Mindset** **internazionale** e **progettualità innovative**
* Iniziative incentrate sul **benessere psicofisico.**

  


***Together for Better – I nostri valori e il nostro impegno***



Leadership, lavoro in team, rispetto di ogni individualità, approfondimento continuo, trasparenza, impegno verso la comunità, integrità: questi sono i **Valori di KPMG** che danno vita a un ambiente aperto allo scambio di idee e di conoscenza, che permette ai nostri professionisti di **esprimere tutto il loro potenziale**.



Grazie all’impegno nel favorire un **ambiente di lavoro inclusivo**, il nostro Network risulta conforme agli standard dei requisiti **ISO 30415**, sul Sistema di Gestione per la Diversità e l’Inclusione, e **UNI PdR 125**, relativa alla parità di genere. All’interno del nostro **Impact Plan** \- *bit.ly/OurImpactPlanKPMG* \- trovi riassunte tutte le **azioni concrete** che stiamo portando avanti su tematiche ambientali, sociali e di governance (**ESG**).


**Unisciti a noi!**


*I tuoi dati saranno trattati nel pieno rispetto delle disposizioni del Regolamento (UE) n. 679/2016 (General Data Protection Regulation \- "GDPR") e successive modifiche. Per ulteriori informazioni, consultare l'informativa sulla privacy di KPMG**.*


***KPMG S.p.A. garantisce le pari opportunità.***

  


*We let the future guide us. Join our next generation*

  


**Do Work That Matters \- Your Role and Responsibilities**



Do you want to do work that is truly meaningful and impactful? At KPMG, you will have the opportunity to help clients, society, and colleagues tackle and solve the most current and complex challenges. In line with the values of integrity and quality, KPMG Audit \& Assurance offers an integrated and constantly evolving portfolio of services, with insights that enable companies to meet the informational needs of the market and investors.



The **Accounting Advisory Services Team**, through **innovation**, ensures that assurance procedures are compliant and attentive to **ESG issues**, increasingly **digitized, integrated, automated**, and based on **Artificial Intelligence and Big Data**. By joining the team, you will collaborate with leading national and international clients in transforming operational and accounting processes and models to make them compliant with regulations and perform well against the company's business model. Your role will focus on strategies, Financial and Regulatory reporting, and analysis of technological platforms supporting CFO processes and organizational change.



Specifically, you will support activities such as:


* Finance Transformation: designing the **accounting model and processes**, defining **business and organizational impacts**, and identifying **target requirements** to support the implementation of IFRS principles (e.g., IFRS 17 and IFRS 9\) and/or following mergers/acquisitions
* Planning and Control: reviewing the **planning and control model and processes**
* Change Management: supporting the definition of new **roles** and **responsibilities** within the organization, identifying **key operational and managerial changes**, and analyzing critical factors underlying the change
* Technologies Change and Smart Digital Finance: developing solutions/platforms and automation processes using Artificial Intelligence and Big Data
* Accounting advice in finance: preparing **financial reporting** required for transaction services projects or extraordinary operations
* Project Management: preparing and monitoring the activity plan, coordinating between Business and IT Stakeholders during implementation phases, and preparing project documentation for Top Management.

  


**Come As You Are \- Your Skills and Characteristics**



This opportunity is perfect for you if:


* You have or are about to obtain a **master’s degree** in **economics/finance**
* You have acquired knowledge of **Italian Accounting Principles** and **IAS/IFRS**
* You have excellent knowledge of the **English language**, preferably supported by study and/or work experiences abroad
* You have excellent knowledge of the **Office suite**.

  


**Your Future With Us \- What You Will Find at KPMG**


* An **inclusive, fair work environment** that respects, encourages, and values **diversity**
* A stimulating and dynamic context that encourages **creativity and teamwork**
* **Hybrid** work mode to promote a healthy work\-life balance
* Advanced **training and development** programs to stay up to date
* **International mindset** and innovative projects
* Initiatives focused on physical and mental **well\-being**.


**Together for Better – Our Values and Commitment**



Leadership, teamwork, respect for every individual, continuous learning, transparency, commitment to the community, integrity: these are **KPMG's Values** that create an environment open to the exchange of ideas and knowledge, allowing our professionals to express their full **potential**. As a result of our commitment to fostering an **inclusive work** environment, our Network complies with the standards of the **ISO 30415** requirements, on the Diversity and Inclusion Management System, and **UNI PdR 125**, relating to gender equality. In our **Impact Plan** \- bit.ly/OurImpactPlanKPMG \- you will find a summary of all the concrete actions we are taking on environmental, social, and governance (**ESG**) issues.


**Join us!**


*Your data will be processed in full compliance with the provisions of Regulation (EU) No. 679/2016 (General Data Protection Regulation \- "GDPR") and subsequent amendments. For more information, please refer to KPMG's privacy policy.*


*KPMG S.p.A. guarantees equal opportunities.*
company_description: At KPMG, you'll be able to tap into your skills, expertise and passion to help organizations find sustainable solutions to the world's most pressing issues.

title: Field Service Engineer
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Able to lift 40 lbs. without assistance**Job Description**

**We are taking open applications for Field Service Engineer roles within the Electron Microscope field!**


At this time, we are open to applications for upcoming roles expected in February/March across Italy, France \& Germany. We are gathering interest for future opportunities across these locations and more broadly across Europe. If you express interest, we will review your application and the Talent Acquisition team may contact you when the role becomes available or sooner. Please note that it might be several weeks until someone contacts you and the first contact is usually via email.

**Field Service Engineer II**

When you join us at Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll be part of an inquisitive team that shares your passion for exploration and discovery. With revenues of more than $40 billion and the largest investment in R\&D in the industry, we give our people the resources and chances to create significant contributions to the world.

**Division Specific Information**


The Materials and Structural Analysis (MSD) Electron Microscopy (EM) business enables customers to find meaningful answers to questions that accelerate breakthrough discoveries and ultimately change the world. The MSD Electron Microscopy (EM) business designs, manufactures, and supports the broadest range of high\-performance microscopy workflow solutions that provide images and answers in the micro\-, nano\-, and picometer scales.

**How You Will Make an Impact**: Field Service Engineers provide technical support and assistance, ensuring the efficient performance of equipment and systems. You will work directly with customers to address their needs, ensuring customer satisfaction and product efficiency.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Manage and complete more complex service tasks independently.
* Ensure compliance with safety and regulatory standards.
* Ensure accurate and detailed service reports are generated.
* Provide expertise and recommendations on technical issues.
* Collaborate with clients to understand their needs and tailor solutions.
* Offer technical training support to Field Service Engineer I.
* Participate in work schedules creation with colleagues and/or management.
* Provide sales leads and feedback to support teams.

**Education**

* Bachelor’s degree or higher in mechatronics, electronics/engineering, or equivalent.

**Experience**

* 2\-5 years of experience in a related field.
* + University degree with some experience of hands\-on technical experience installing, troubleshooting and repairing complex analytical instruments, ideally of Electron\-Microscopes or similar technology.

**Knowledge, Skills, Abilities**

* Analytical troubleshooting and problem\-solving abilities.
* Interest in technology and IT trends.
* Knowledge of electron microscopes is an advantage.

**Physical Requirements / Work Environment**

Excellent hand\-eye coordination and manual dexterity.

**Benefits**


We offer competitive remuneration, annual incentive plan bonus, healthcare, and a range of employee benefits. Thermo Fisher Scientific offers employment with an innovative, forward\-thinking organization, and outstanding career and development prospects. We offer an exciting company culture that stands for integrity, intensity, involvement, and innovation!
company_description: At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our professionals develop critical solutions—and build rewarding careers.

title: Corporate Coverage Analyst - GCIB
description:
**Do you want your voice heard and your actions to count?**

Discover your opportunity with Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s leading financial groups. Across the globe, we’re 150,000 colleagues, striving to make a difference for every client, organization, and community we serve. We stand for our values, building long\-term relationships, serving society, and fostering shared and sustainable growth for a better world.


With a vision to be the world’s most trusted financial group, it’s part of our culture to put people first, listen to new and diverse ideas and collaborate toward greater innovation, speed and agility. This means investing in talent, technologies, and tools that empower you to own your career.


Join MUFG, where being inspired is expected and making a meaningful impact is rewarded.

**About us:**


GCIB Italy manages MUFG’s relationships with leading Italian corporates, financial institutions, financial sponsors, Italian subsidiaries of multinational groups and Italian companies owned by private equity or infrastructure funds.


Leveraging MUFG’s global network and comprehensive product platform, together with our product specialists we support clients across corporate finance, capital markets, structured solutions, leveraged and project finance and strategic advisory needs.

**Role Overview:**


We are seeking a highly motivated Analyst to join our Global Corporate \& Investment Banking team in Milan. This role represents the first step in a long\-term career path toward senior client coverage or product origination positions.


The Analyst will be fully integrated into the team from day one, working closely with Senior Bankers and Product Specialists and contributing directly to client activities, deal execution, and strategic initiatives.

**Key Responsibilities:**


1\. Client Coverage \& Business Origination

* Support Senior Bankers in daily client interaction, contributing to idea generation and origination across a wide range of products.
* Assist in identifying new business opportunities and support onboarding of prospective clients.
* Prepare high\-quality marketing materials, pitch books, and presentations for client meetings.
* Contribute to the development and execution of Account Plans, ensuring cross\-sell opportunities are maximized.
* Draft clear and accurate call reports to maintain strong internal documentation and follow\-up.


2\. Deal Assessment \& Execution

* Develop a solid understanding of credit fundamentals across corporate finance, leveraged finance, project finance and fund finance.
* Prepare Deal Screening Memos, business cases and internal submissions in collaboration with Senior Bankers and Product Partners.
* Act as first point of contact for internal procedures, supporting execution processes including documentation coordination, checklists, compliance requirements and KYC.


3\. Risk, Governance \& Internal Reporting

* Apply MUFG’s risk culture and support the risk assessment framework through thoughtful analysis and disciplined documentation.
* Gain ownership of KYC, onboarding processes and internal reporting requirements.
* Ensure full compliance with internal standards, governance requirements and regulatory expectations.

**What We Are Looking For**


Education \& Experience

* Final\-year students or recent graduates (up to 12 months) are encouraged to apply.
* Candidates with up to 2 years of experience in a top\-tier financial institution are welcome; experience in Debt Advisory, Leveraged Finance, Project Finance, or related fields is an advantage but not essential.
* Internal applicants with the right motivation and skills will also be considered.


Skills \& Competencies

* Strong motivation to work in a client\-facing, high\-performance environment.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate across multiple teams.
* Proactive mindset, intellectual curiosity and strong commitment to continuous learning.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment and deliver high\-quality output under time pressure.
* Team\-player mentality combined with the ability to develop independent insights and propose thoughtful solutions.
* High proficiency with Microsoft Office tools and strong analytical capabilities.
* Fluency in Italian and English is required; additional European languages or Japanese are a plus.


Culture \& Values


Client Centric

* Always ask the question “how does this impact our Clients?”
* Think like our Clients and advocate for their success.
* Listen to the voice of the Client to drive innovation.
* Be a trusted advisor by providing thoughtful, integrated solutions to meet our Client’s unique needs.


People Focused

* Connect beyond your Team and leverage our global strength as One MUFG.
* Value contributions and results over titles and hierarchy.
* Respect each other, assume good intent, and listen reflectively.
* Develop yourself and others, learn from mistakes and recognise achievements.


Listen Up. Speak Up

* Actively listen to diverse perspectives before voicing opinions or making decisions.
* Ask the difficult questions to stretch our thinking and expand our mind\-set.
* Call out behaviours that don’t align with our values, conduct and culture.
* Propose innovative ideas even when they may be unpopular.


Innovate \& Simplify

* Be resilient and challenge the status quo to support transformation and innovation.
* Simplify what does not need to be complex.
* Be curious about what’s next and doing it better.
* Expect efficiency and manage Company resources as if they were your own.


Own \& Execute

* Execute with speed, simplicity and transparency to manage risk and drive results.
* Empower those closest to the issue to solve it.
* Be accountable for your actions and accept responsibility.
* Pro\-actively prioritize competing objectives to deliver high performance.

**What We Offer**


Joining GCIB Italy means becoming part of a high\-performing, globally connected platform where talent is recognised, supported and accelerated. We offer:

* A steep learning curve and meaningful responsibility from day one, with direct exposure to senior bankers, product experts and top\-tier clients.
* A structured development path, including technical and soft\-skill training, mentorship, on\-the\-job learning and access to MUFG’s global knowledge network.
* Broad client and product exposure, across corporate lending, leveraged finance, project finance, structured solutions and international coverage teams.
* A collaborative and inclusive culture, where individual contribution is valued and teamwork is at the core of success.
* Career growth opportunities, with a long\-term development track designed to progress toward Associate and Senior roles within Client Coverage or Product Origination.
* International perspective, through interaction with MUFG colleagues across EMEA, the Americas and Asia.
* Competitive compensation and benefits, aligned with the standards of leading global financial institutions.
* A modern hybrid working model (1 day per week remote), designed to support productivity, personal development and team cohesion.


We are open to considering flexible working requests in line with organisational requirements.


MUFG is committed to embracing diversity and building an inclusive culture where all employees are valued, respected and their opinions count. We support the principles of equality, diversity and inclusion in recruitment and employment, and oppose all forms of discrimination on the grounds of age, sex, gender, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy and maternity, race, gender reassignment, religion or belief and marriage or civil partnership.


We make our recruitment decisions in a non\-discriminatory manner in accordance with our commitment to identifying the right skills for the right role and our obligations under the law.

title: Consultant - SAP FI
description:
*The chance to build a better future is right in front of you*  


**Do Work That Matters \- Il tuo ruolo e le tue responsabilità**  



Vuoi fare un lavoro che sia davvero significativo e di impatto? In KPMG avrai l’opportunità di aiutare i clienti, la società, le colleghe e i colleghi ad affrontare e risolvere le sfide più attuali e complesse. KPMG Advisory offre servizi di consulenza per affiancare le organizzazioni nella trasformazione dei processi, nella digitalizzazione e nell’adozione di soluzioni SAP a supporto del cambiamento.

In particolare, entrando a far parte del Team **Enterprise Platforms**, sarai coinvolta/o in progetti di implementazione in ambito **SAP FI** (**Financial Accounting**) per clienti del **Settore Pubblico**, contribuendo all’analisi dei requisiti e alladefinizione della soluzione a supporto dei processi amministrativo\-contabili e delle attività di chiusura.

  



Nello specifico, ti occuperai di:

  


* Raccolta e analisi dei requisiti attraverso **workshop** e **interviste** con le strutture Business e IT del cliente.
* Supporto al disegno del modello to\-be e alla **progettazione** della soluzione SAP più idonea, in coerenza con i processi e i vincoli del contesto pubblico.
* Configurazione (customizing) di **SAP FI** e collaborazione con il team progettuale per garantire la coerenza della soluzione end\-to\-end, incluse eventuali integrazioni con altri moduli o sistemi.
* Redazione e aggiornamento della **documentazione funzionale** e tecnico\-funzionale, comprendenteprocessi, specifiche, tracciabilità requisiti.
* Esecuzione e supporto alle **fasi di test** (SIT/UAT), supporto **alle attività di** cutover, go\-live e stabilizzazione post avvio.
* Supporto, ove previsto, alle attività di **preparazione** e m**igrazione dei dati,** nonché alle **riconciliazioni** contabili in ambito Finance.

  




Lavorerai a stretto contatto con clienti del **settore pubblico**, in contesti di Pubblica Amministrazione centrale e locale, enti regolatori, sanità, istruzione e società a partecipazione pubblica.

  


**Come As You Are \- Le tue competenze e caratteristiche**



Questa opportunità è perfetta per te se:

  


* Hai maturato **2–4 anni di esperienza** su progetti **implementativi in ambito** **SAP** con focus su **SAP FI**
* Hai maturato esperienza nelle attivitàdi analisi dei requisiti, supporto al disegno della soluzione, customizing, esecuzione dei test e supporto al go‑live in **progetti di implementazione** o **rollout**.
* Possiedi una buona conoscenza dei principali ambiti di **configurazione** FI (FI/GL, FI/AP, FI/AR, FI/AA); l’esposizione alle **integrazioni** con CO, MM e SD rappresenta un plus.
* Buona conoscenza della lingua **inglese**.

  


**Your Future With Us \- Cosa troverai in KPMG**  


* Un ambiente di lavoro **inclusivo**, **equo** e che rispetta, incoraggia e valorizza le diversità.
* Un contesto **stimolante** e **dinamico** che incentiva la creatività e il lavoro di squadra.
* **Modalità di lavoro ibrida**, per promuovere un sano equilibrio tra vita lavorativa e personale.
* Programmi di **formazione** e **sviluppo** avanzati per rimanere sempre aggiornati.
* **Mindset internazionale** e progettualità innovative.
* Iniziative incentrate sul **benessere psicofisico**.

  


**Together for Better – I nostri valori e il nostro impegno**  



Leadership, lavoro in team, rispetto di ogni individualità, approfondimento continuo, trasparenza, impegno verso la comunità, integrità: questi sono i Valori di KPMG che danno vita a un ambiente aperto allo scambio di idee e di conoscenza, che permette ai nostri professionisti di esprimere tutto il loro potenziale.

Grazie all’impegno nel favorire un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo, il nostro Network risulta conforme agli standard dei requisiti ISO 30415, sul Sistema di Gestione per la Diversità e l’Inclusione, e UNI PdR 125, relativa alla parità di genere. All’interno del nostro Impact Plan \- Our Impact Plan (kpmg.com) \- trovi riassunte tutte le azioni concrete che stiamo portando avanti su tematiche ambientali, sociali e di governance (**ESG**).  


**Unisciti a noi!**  



I tuoi dati saranno trattati nel pieno rispetto delle disposizioni del Regolamento (UE) n. 679/2016 (General Data Protection Regulation \- "GDPR") e successive modifiche. Per ulteriori informazioni, consultare l'informativa sulla privacy di KPMG.  


**KPMG Advisory S.p.A. garantisce le pari opportunità.**  


*The chance to build a better future is right in front of you*

  


**Do Work That Matters – Your Role and Responsibilities**

  



Do you want a job that is truly meaningful and impactful? At **KPMG**, you’ll have the opportunity to help clients, society, and your colleagues tackle and solve today’s most pressing and complex challenges. **KPMG Advisory** provides consulting services to support organizations in process transformation, digitalization, and the adoption of SAP solutions that enable change.

  



In particular, by joining the **Enterprise Platforms Team**, you will be involved in **SAP FI (Financial Accounting)** implementation projects for **Public Sector clients**, contributing to requirements analysis and to defining solutions that support administrative and accounting processes and period\-end closing activities.

  



Specifically, you will be responsible for:

  


* **Gathering** and **analyzing requirements** through workshops and interviews with the client’s Business and IT teams.
* **Supporting the design** of the target operating model (to\-be) and the definition of the most suitable SAP solution, in line with processes and constraints typical of the public sector.
* **Configuring** (customizing) **SAP FI** and collaborating with the project team to ensure end\-to\-end solution consistency, including any integrations with other modules or systems.
* **Producing** and **maintaining** functional and techno\-functional documentation, including process documentation, specifications, and requirements traceability.
* **Executing** and **supporting testing phases** (SIT/UAT), as well as supporting cutover, go\-live, and post go\-live stabilization activities.
* Supporting, where required, **data preparation** and migration activities, as well as Finance\-related accounting reconciliations.

  




You will work closely with **Public Sector** clients in contexts such as central and local Public Administration, regulatory bodies, healthcare, education, and publicly owned companies.

  


**Come As You Are – Your Skills and Characteristics**

  



This opportunity is a great fit for you if:

  


* You have **2–4 years** of experience on SAP implementation projects with a focus on **SAP FI**.
* You have experience in requirements **analysis**, solution **design** support, **customizing**, test execution, and **go\-live** support within implementation or rollout projects.
* You have a solid knowledge of the main **FI configuration areas** (FI/GL, FI/AP, FI/AR, FI/AA); exposure to integrations with CO, MM, and SD is a plus.
* You have a good command of **English**.

  




**Your Future With Us – What You’ll Find at KPMG**

  


* An **inclusive** and fair working environment that respects, encourages, and values diversity.
* A **stimulating** and **dynamic** setting that fosters creativity and teamwork.
* A **hybrid working** model to promote a healthy work–life balance.
* Advanced **training** and **development** programs to keep your skills up to date.
* An **international mindset** and innovative projects.
* Initiatives focused on **psychological** and **physical wellbeing**.

  




**Together for Better – Our Values and Commitment**

  



Leadership, teamwork, respect for each individual, continuous learning, transparency, commitment to the community, and integrity: these are KPMG’s Values, which foster an environment open to the exchange of ideas and knowledge and enable our professionals to express their full potential.

  



Thanks to our commitment to creating an inclusive workplace, our Network complies with the ISO 30415 standards for Diversity \& Inclusion Management Systems and the UNI PdR 125 standard relating to gender equality. Within our Impact Plan – Our Impact Plan (kpmg.com) – you can find a summary of the concrete actions we are taking across environmental, social, and governance (ESG) topics.

  


**Join us!**

  



Your personal data will be processed in full compliance with Regulation (EU) No. 679/2016 (General Data Protection Regulation – “GDPR”) and subsequent amendments. For further information, please refer to KPMG’s privacy notice.

  


**KPMG Advisory S.p.A. is an equal opportunity employer.**

title: Venture Capital Analyst
description:
**Sella Direct Ventures** is the venture capital arm of **Sella Group**, one of the largest private and independent banking group in Italy. We have over 5,000 employees, we are present in 7 countries, and we have 300 branches throughout Italy. Our activities include commercial and retail banking, private and wealth management, corporate \& investment banking, payment systems, open banking, and information technology services.

**Sella Direct** **Ventures** operates with a single\-LP structure — with Sella Group as our sole investor — combining the discipline and deal\-making approach of an independent VC with the strategic resources, network, and domain expertise of a major financial institution.


We back early\-stage startups building the future of financial services, covering the full fintech spectrum, from embedded finance to payments and financial infrastructure. We operate as a lean, high\-conviction fund with a European focus and a global mindset. We can act as a strategic partner — not just a capital provider — for the founders we back.


We are looking for a driven and intellectually curious **Analyst** to join our team.

**The Role**


As an Analyst at Sella Direct Ventures, you will be a core part of a small, ambitious team where your work has real impact from day one. You will be involved across the full investment lifecycle — from identifying and evaluating new opportunities to supporting portfolio companies as they scale.


This is a generalist role with a strong bias toward analytical rigor, intellectual honesty, and genuine curiosity about where financial services are headed.

**What You'll Do**

**Deal Sourcing \& Evaluation**

* Proactively identify, map, and track high\-potential startups across our investment themes
* Build and maintain relationships with founders, co\-investors, and the broader European VC ecosystem
* Attend industry events, demos, and conferences to strengthen deal flow and market knowledge

**Investment Process**

* Lead and support due diligence across all dimensions: market sizing, competitive landscape, business model analysis, unit economics, and team assessment
* Build financial models to evaluate deal structures and returns
* Prepare investment memos and present recommendations to the Investment Committee

**Market \& Thematic Research**

* Develop and maintain investment theses across key verticals and emerging trends
* Monitor regulatory developments, market dynamics, and technology shifts relevant to our focus areas
* Produce internal research reports and sector deep\-dives

**Portfolio Support**

* Assist portfolio companies with strategic introductions, hiring, and access to the Sella network
* Support reporting and monitoring of portfolio performance

**What We're Looking For**

**Experience**

* 1–3 years in venture capital, growth equity, investment banking, strategy consulting, or a high\-growth startup/scaleup
* Direct exposure to financial services or fintech is a strong plus, though not a strict requirement

**Skills**

* Strong analytical and structured thinking — you can break down complex problems and communicate them clearly
* Financial modelling proficiency and comfort with both quantitative and qualitative analysis
* Excellent written and verbal communication in both **English** and **Italian**
* High proficiency with MS Office / Google Workspace and familiarity with AI tools

**Mindset**

* Genuine passion for technology, startups, and the future of financial services
* Intellectually curious — you read broadly, form opinions, and enjoy being challenged
* Proactive and autonomous — you take ownership without waiting to be told what to do
* A natural relationship\-builder who engages authentically with founders, investors, and operators
* Comfortable in a lean, fast\-moving environment where priorities shift and ambiguity is the norm

**What We Offer**

* A front\-row seat to the European fintech and venture ecosystem
* Exposure to the full investment cycle within an active, well\-networked team
* Direct access to and mentorship from senior leaders across Sella Group
* A genuine voice in investment decisions from early on
* The opportunity to build your network across founders, co\-investors, and operators across Europe
* A collaborative and intellectually stimulating environment
* Apprenticeship contract with structured learning and on\-the\-job development, in an innovative and inclusive culture that values ideas and continuous improvement.
* Flexible home office (i.e., smart working) up to 13 days/month on average and additional paid leave to support work–life balance.
* Comprehensive total rewards package including meal vouchers, performance\-related bonus, flexible welfare, supplementary pension scheme, and preferential banking \& insurance conditions.
* Strong focus on wellbeing, with private health and life insurance, mental and physical wellbeing services, and access to a benefits platform with discounts and offers.

*Sella Direct Ventures \& the Sella Group are committed to building an inclusive and sustainable work environment. We believe diverse perspectives make us better investors and partners, and we actively welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds.*


\#LI\-AF1 \#LI\-Hybrid
